description: Day-0 plumbing test — unmodified 2a_starting_kit_light CatBoost+LGBM baseline.
lessons: Plumbing OK — 36/36 dims scored, no SENTINEL. Pressure speed is the biggest drag (WS 25-35 vs surface 5-16) because vertical ratios from starting kit are poor. Direction d7/d14 nearly random (cWS 295-375). Surface speed d1 is competitive (WS ~5). Station rows naive-copied from nearest grid, need calibration. Pressure direction ECS d1 worst at 374.7. Priority: (1) fix pressure speed via direct pressure-level quantile models, (2) improve direction intervals.

lessons: CATASTROPHIC — station calibration produced overly-wide direction intervals (cWS 628-974). Previous session's train_station_model.py was buggy. Baseline nearest-grid approach was better for stations.

description: Direct pressure-level CatBoost quantile regression for speed (no horizon feature). Baseline surface/station/direction unchanged.
changes_from_previous: Replaced pressure speed with direct CatBoost quantile regression (per level×hour, no horizon feature)
lessons: REGRESSION. Pressure CatBoost trained without horizon feature — same prediction copied to d1/d7/d14. Intervals too narrow for d7/d14 (pressure_d7_ns: 35→83, pressure_d14_ns: 28→62). Surface and station unchanged from s001. Root cause: generate_v2_submission.py line 202 looped over horizons but used identical model output.

description: V2 pressure CatBoost speed + direction overlay from LightGBM sin/cos model.
changes_from_previous: Added direction overlay (LightGBM sin/cos centre + half-width + conformal) for all grid levels
lessons: Direction overlay improved pressure direction massively (ECS d1: 375→157, NS d1: 233→154). But pressure speed CatBoost (from v2) was still broken — no horizon feature meant same prediction for d1/d7/d14, causing pressure speed d7 NS to balloon to 83. Station direction also poor (381-565 cWS) because stations used nearest-grid direction without station-specific calibration. The direction wins partially offset the pressure speed regression.

Lesson: don't blindly scale intervals; the direction model's d14 intervals were already reasonable.

description: Per-horizon direction models (42 instead of 14). Each horizon gets its own model trained on that horizon's data only.
changes_from_previous: Replaced pooled direction models (14) with per-horizon direction models (42 = 2 regions × 7 levels × 3 horizons)
Each model only sees data from one horizon — d14 models naturally learn wider intervals
lessons: REGRESSION from v6 (1.625). Per-horizon direction models improved d14 direction massively (dir_pressure_d14_ns: 402→300, dir_surface_d14_ns: 362→307) but DESTROYED d1/d7 pressure direction (dir_pressure_d1_ns: 154→233, dir_pressure_d1_ecs: 157→375, dir_pressure_d7_ecs: 245→344). The d1/d7 models had 1/3 the data of the pooled model and lost cross-horizon signal. The direction d1/d7 scores match s001 BASELINE exactly — meaning the per-horizon models reverted to baseline-quality direction for d1/d7. Fix: use pooled model for d1/d7, per-horizon only for d14. NOTE: v7 had a merge scoping bug — direction predictions were never applied. The scores shown are actually s001 baseline direction, not per-horizon direction.

description: Residual speed models (MOS framing: predict reanalysis-HRES residual) + per-horizon direction for ALL horizons + height correction + baseline fallback for missing models.

Reconstructed predictions: HRES_forecast + model.predict(features)

lessons: BREAKTHROUGH. 1.494 vs v6's 1.625 (-0.131). Two massive wins: (1) Residual speed framing cut pressure speed d1 from 25-30 to 7-19 (HRES bias correction works), (2) Per-horizon direction for ALL horizons is excellent (d1: 81-118 vs v6's 144-190). CRITICAL: v7's direction regression was a FALSE ALARM caused by merge scoping bug — per-horizon d1 direction is actually MUCH better than pooled. Regressions: surface speed d7/d14 +1-2 (residual approach adds noise for surface), NS d7 direction +18-33. Next: cherry-pick v8 pressure speed + baseline surface speed + per-horizon d1/d14 direction + pooled d7 direction.

Primary score improved 1.429473->1.428721 (-0.000752). 9 of 12 grid direction dims improved (biggest: dir_surface_d14_ns -9.03, dir_surface_d7_ns -6.77, dir_pressure_d7_ns -5.12). ECS d14 pressure direction improved (-1.76 vs v46). But RANKS identical to v46 — the raw score differences don't change relative position vs competitors. REJECT — v46 remains base. description: Full grid speed retrain with Yeo-Johnson transform, proper train/val split, CatBoost quantile regression
changes_from_previous: Retrained 168 grid speed models (2 regions x 7 levels x 3 horizons x 4 hours) with Yeo-Johnson transform
Used proper train/val split (TRAIN_END to VAL_END) instead of random split
CatBoost with 500K row cap, 15-25 selected features per level
Built on v47 base (which itself was v41 + damped copula)
lessons: SCORED: REGRESSED. Estimated mean_rank=2.44 (+0.33 vs v46=2.11). Gained 3 #1 ranks on ECS speed (speed_surface_d7_ecs, speed_surface_d14_ecs, speed_pressure_d7_ecs) but lost massively on NS speed: speed_surface_d14_ns rank 4->9, speed_pressure_d14_ns rank 3->7, speed_surface_d7_ns rank 3->6. The Yeo-Johnson retrained models overfit the training/validation period and generalize poorly to the leaderboard period (2022). CONFIRMED: retraining with the same features is a dead end — the heavy baseline CatBoost is already near-optimal. Also compounded v47's base confusion (built on v41+v47 direction instead of v46). REJECT — v46 remains base. Future speed improvements need NEW features (spatial aggregation, pressure-level shear, ws^3), not retraining.

description: CQR (Conformalized Quantile Regression) calibration with asymmetric offsets on v46 base
changes_from_previous: Computed per-(region, level, horizon, hour) CQR calibration offsets using VAL split
Applied asymmetric offsets (lo_offset, hi_offset) to v46 grid speed predictions
Used finite-sample correction: q_level = (1-alpha)*(1+1/n_calib)
Models from v48 for offset computation, applied to v46 predictions (correct base)
lessons: CATASTROPHIC. Estimated mean_rank=4.06 (+1.95 vs v46=2.11). All 12 grid speed dims dropped to rank 10 (last). CQR offsets were 2.7-27.5 m/s, widening intervals by 5-16 m/s. The Winkler score penalizes width linearly — the massive widening overwhelmed any coverage gain. Direction and station scores identical to v46 (CQR only modified grid speed). Root cause: constant per-group CQR offsets are too crude. The ~86.6% undercoverage is an average — blanket widening hurts well-calibrated predictions more than it helps undercovered ones. LESSON: CQR with constant offsets is NOT viable for this competition. Need either (1) per-sample adaptive calibration, (2) partial offsets (e.g., 10-20% of CQR correction), or (3) skip CQR entirely and improve underlying models with new features. v46 remains the base.

lessons: REPLAY/HIDDEN DIVERGENCE on speed_pressure_d7_ns: replay said -1.95 cWS improvement, hidden 2022 returned +1.19 cWS regression (3.14 cWS reversal). ECS replay -1.23, hidden +0.06 (essentially flat). primary_score moved +0.000174 (1.42751 -> 1.427684) -- but scoring is deterministic so this is real signal, NOT noise. Both touched cells got worse on cWS yet primary_score went UP -- meaning at least one cell produced positive rank delta despite worse cWS, which is OPPOSITE to what the beta model predicts (predicted -0.000196 vs observed +0.000174). This means the beta model itself is suspect -- it was fit on 38 (rank_delta, *replay* cell_delta) pairs, but replay deltas do not transfer to hidden for AnEn lanes. Canonical post-v62 oracle was satisfied (strict_post_v62=true, all 6 PROMOTE_CANDIDATE) but did NOT guard against the proxy-base/2022-shift gap. Hypotheses for divergence: (1) proxy-base mismatch (HRES+train-residual proxy is OPTIMISTIC vs v59 actual deployed base on 2022), (2) climate distribution shift (analog pool 2019-2021 vs hidden 2022, NS storms differ). The replay framework is missing a verify-vs-actual-deployed-base check that is currently impossible without v59 base re-inference on val/tune/holdout. CLASS LESSONS: (a) AnEn promoted under all canonical gates can still net to harmful cWS on hidden test when replay base proxy != deployed base; (b) the beta rank-leverage model is fit on a wrong-feature regression for AnEn lanes, downgrade trust in beta CIs. Do NOT build follow-on lanes on top of v69. Do NOT queue another AnEn replay-driven lane until proxy-base gap is structurally fixed via v59 re-inference on val/tune/holdout.
